title: Set up Prisma schema, PostgreSQL database, and seed scripts
description: Establish the base database schema with roles, users, and core tables. Create seed script for default roles (ADMIN, HR, MANAGER, EMPLOYEE) and a default admin user.
frontendWork: None
backendWork: Initialize Prisma client, create schema.prisma, set up db-backup.sh, and write seed.ts script.
databaseWork: Create PostgreSQL tables, constraints, and indexes.
testingWork: Verify seed script creates records in DB and migrations run successfully.
expectedOutput: Working database schema, Prisma client, and successful seeding of default data.

title: Implement core JWT Authentication and Role guards
description: Implement POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/logout, and GET /api/auth/me. Write JWT verification and role-based route protection middleware on the backend.
frontendWork: Create LoginPage component, setup AuthContext, and secure routes in AppRouter.
backendWork: Write login, logout, me controllers, bcrypt password verification, JWT sign, and authenticate/role guard middleware.
databaseWork: Query User table and return linked Role.
testingWork: Unit tests for login, role guard blocking, and token validation.
expectedOutput: Secure login flow, authenticated sessions, and route guards active.

title: Implement Refresh Token Rotation and Secure Cookie Storage
description: Store refresh tokens in RefreshTokens database table. Deliver refresh tokens in HTTP-only, secure cookies. Implement POST /api/auth/refresh and enforce token revocation on logout.
frontendWork: Update axios interceptor to handle token refresh and automatic logout on session expiration.
backendWork: Implement /api/auth/refresh endpoint, token rotation logic, token hashing, and secure cookie configuration.
databaseWork: Create RefreshTokens table, manage relations, and delete tokens on revocation.
testingWork: Verify token rotation, invalid refresh rejection, and concurrent login stability.
expectedOutput: Silent token refresh active and secure cookie storage implemented.

title: Implement Employee CRUD APIs and basic layout
description: Create Employee table in DB. Write backend endpoints for employee listing, detail, creation, status updates, and soft delete.
frontendWork: Build EmployeesPage, EmployeeTable, and EmployeeForm for adding/editing records.
backendWork: Write employee routes, controllers for CRUD, validation using Zod, and manager reporting logic.
databaseWork: Create Employee table with unique constraints on employeeCode and userId.
testingWork: Test email uniqueness, code uniqueness, and soft delete flag updates.
expectedOutput: Admin and HR can manage employee profiles and view their details.

title: Implement Employee Profile tabs and document upload systems
description: Implement profile tabs for overview, attendance, leaves, payroll, and documents. Enable profile picture uploads and document management with file metadata in DB.
frontendWork: Create EmployeeProfilePage, AvatarUploadModal, and EmployeeDocumentsTab.
backendWork: Configure multer storage, implement document upload routes, and link file path references in database.
databaseWork: Create EmployeeDocument table linked to Employee.
testingWork: Verify mime-type validations, file size constraints, and file storage retrieval.
expectedOutput: Uploaded documents are saved to uploads/ folder and metadata is correctly linked in DB.

title: Implement Attendance Check-in, Check-out, and Break sessions
description: Implement daily check-in and check-out APIs. Calculate work hours and break duration server-side. Enforce one record per day and prevent checkout before checkin.
frontendWork: Build check-in/check-out toggle, break timer, and monthly calendar history widget.
backendWork: Write check-in, check-out, break start, break end controllers, late check-in calculations, and duration summation.
databaseWork: Create Attendance and BreakSession tables, unique index on (employeeId, attendanceDate).
testingWork: Test check-in/out order, duplicate check-in rejection, and correct duration counting.
expectedOutput: Employees can mark their attendance and break sessions with accurate server-side timing.

title: Implement Attendance Regularization and Manager reviews
description: Allow employees to submit regularization requests for missing check-ins or check-outs. Managers can approve or reject, which directly updates the attendance record.
frontendWork: Build AttendanceRequestsPage with submission forms and approval/rejection tables.
backendWork: Write request creation, manager approval/rejection endpoints, and auto-update of attendance records.
databaseWork: Create AttendanceRegularizationRequest table with index on employeeId and status.
testingWork: Verify that approved requests update check-in/out times in Attendance table, and rejected requests write a reason.
expectedOutput: Regularization workflow functions, modifying attendance logs on manager approval.

title: Implement Leave balance check and manager/HR approval workflow
description: Implement leave types (yearly, quarterly) and enforce balances. When applying for leave, validate date overlap and balance availability. Require manager approval, followed by HR approval.
frontendWork: Build LeavesPage with balance cards, apply form, and manager review tab.
backendWork: Write leave application routes, overlap checks, balance checks, and approval-step updates. Debit balances only on final HR approval.
databaseWork: Create LeaveType, LeaveBalance, LeaveRequest tables; unique index on (employeeId, leaveTypeId, year).
testingWork: Test overlapping leave rejection, balance exhaustion rejection, and balance debiting upon approval.

title: Implement Leave Medical Proof uploading and HR review rules
description: Automatically flag leaves longer than 3 days as requiring medical proof. Require upload of medical files and allow HR to approve or reject them.

backendWork: Implement medical proof attachment fields in LeaveRequest, validation checks, and review APIs.
databaseWork: Add medicalProofStatus, medicalProofSubmittedAt, and file path fields to LeaveRequest.
testingWork: Verify that leaves requiring medical proof are blocked from final approval until uploaded and reviewed.

title: Implement Monthly Draft Payroll Generation

testingWork: Test monthly payroll record uniqueness and basic calculation logic.

title: Implement Overtime session tracking and verifications

title: Implement Incentives management and approvals

title: Implement Payroll finalization and publishing controls

testingWork: Verify that a finalized payroll cannot be modified, and employees can only see their own published payslips.
expectedOutput: Lock button freezes editing; employees can download their respective payslip PDFs.

title: Implement Google Workspace OAuth linkage and token storage

title: Automate Google Calendar Out-Of-Office blocking on Leave approvals

testingWork: Compile standalone release executable, launch and test local connection, verify automated break creation on system locking/idleness.

testingWork: Manual verification of raising dispute, HR review, approval points reversal, and database consistency.

description: Factor in late check-in penalty minutes when displaying overtime in the attendance history table to prevent calculation discrepancies with the dashboard workday timeline.
frontendWork: Update renderOvertime in AttendancePage.tsx to calculate dynamic required minutes as 540 + penaltyMinutes and check worked minutes against it.
backendWork: None
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify overtime column accurately accounts for late check-in penalties on the Attendance page.
expectedOutput: Overtime column in history table correctly accounts for late penalties and shows accurate durations.

title: Implement Net Worked Minutes and Crash-Aware Concluding Scheduler
description: Subtract break durations from workedMinutes at the database level during checkout/finalization. Reschedule the automated attendance finalization to 7:00 AM daily and implement telemetry-based crash vs clean shutdown distinction. Overhaul the frontend Day Summary to replace Events Logged with Uptime and Active Penalties cards.
frontendWork: Update WorkdayTimeline.tsx to calculate and display Uptime (since last break/away) and Active Penalties, replacing the old Events Logged card.
backendWork: Refactor routes.ts and scheduler.ts to compute net productive time by subtracting break sessions, remove immediate checkout on SHUTDOWN, and implement telemetry analysis in the 7:00 AM daily finalization job.
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify net productive time calculations, 7:00 AM scheduler crash detection, and Day Summary cards.
expectedOutput: Productive Time displays net worked hours, the 7:00 AM scheduler correctly handles PC crashes without penalizing employees, and the Day Summary shows Uptime and Active Penalties.

title: Unify Projects under The Star Prime (TSP)

frontendWork: Implement tabs switcher for received vs assigned tasks, support dual fetching logic, conditionally render action buttons, and display creator/assignee metadata.
backendWork: Verify compatibility of existing /tasks and /tasks/manager APIs.
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Manual verification of tab switching, task toggling, and role-based visibility.
expectedOutput: Managers and team leads can toggle between tasks assigned to them and tasks they assigned to others, with full completion workflows.

description: Fix live status determination by fetching active break sessions where endTime is null and marking employees on break as AWAY. Synchronize frontend with a break-updated event listener in AppProvider for instant status updates.
frontendWork: Add event listener to AppProvider context to instantly trigger refreshLiveStatuses and refreshSummary upon break-updated event.
backendWork: Modify live-status route to select active break sessions for today and set employee status to AWAY if active break session exists.

expectedOutput: Web-initiated break sessions correctly set status to AWAY and update Navbar, Sidebar status dots instantly.

title: Implement Paid Overtime Pre-Approval Workflow and Paid Tags
description: Allow employees to submit overtime pre-approval requests before 5:00 PM on working days. Approved requests are classified as Paid Overtime and display a 'Paid' tag next to the hours in the Attendance History table.
frontendWork: Add Request Paid Overtime button with time cutoff checking, pre-approval reason modal, and Paid tag rendering next to verified overtime hours in the history table.
backendWork: Implement POST /attendance/overtime/pre-approval endpoint with time cutoff and calendar exceptions validation. Update start and verify routes to handle pre-approved sessions.
databaseWork: Add APPROVED to OvertimeStatus enum and isPaid boolean to OvertimeSession model.
testingWork: Verify database schema changes, route permissions and cutoffs, button visibility rules, and rendering of the 'Paid' tags.
expectedOutput: Employees can request paid overtime pre-approval before 5 PM, which displays a Paid tag upon verification.

title: Implement Incomplete Shift Hours Warning on Checkout and Logout
description: Add proactive warnings to alert employees who try to checkout of their shift or log out of the application before completing their required working hours (9 hours + late check-in penalties). Shows a prominent red card inside the checkout modal and a browser confirm alert upon app logout.
frontendWork: Add isShiftIncomplete and remainingMins calculations. Render a red warning banner inside the Checkout Modal in AttendanceQuickAction.tsx, and integrate a descriptive confirm warning in the Navbar.tsx logout handler.
backendWork: None
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify warning calculations under different check-in times and late penalties. Assert red banner visibility inside checkout modal and alert message on app logout.
expectedOutput: Employees receive detailed bold red warnings and confirm alerts if they attempt to checkout or log out early.

title: Implement Dynamic Shift Management System
description: Provide a complete dynamic Shift Management system where an Admin can configure work shifts (start/end times, required hours, grace periods) and switch employees between shifts. Non-admin roles are barred from accessing these features.
frontendWork: Create ShiftManagementPage with active shift cards and bulk employee shift switcher. Secure sidebar link for ADMIN role only.
backendWork: Modify GET /api/employees to include shift relation. Backend routes, models, and shift-aware penalties/overtime calculations are fully integrated.
databaseWork: None (Shift schema and migrations completed earlier)
apiWork: GET /api/shifts, POST /api/shifts, PUT /api/shifts/:id, DELETE /api/shifts/:id, POST /api/shifts/assign, GET /api/employees
testingWork: Verify admin-restricted access, shift creation, bulk switching, and shift-aware check-in/out and overtime logic.
expectedOutput: Admin can seamlessly create shifts and assign employees, with dynamic attendance and overtime logic reacting to the assigned shift timings.

title: Implement Shift-Specific Break Configurations & Customizable Timing Windows
description: Provide shift-specific break toggles (hasBreaks, allowMorningTea, allowLunch, allowEveningTea, allowDinner) and customizable start/end timing windows for each break type, including a 40-minute Dinner Break (20:00 - 22:00 IST) for night shifts. Restrict Lunch to morning shifts and Dinner to night shifts.
frontendWork: Add checkboxes and inline start/end timing inputs (HH:MM) to the Shift modal, automatically sync Lunch/Dinner toggles with shift start time, and display allowed breaks with their timing ranges on shift cards.
backendWork: Enforce shift break toggles and custom timings dynamically in /break/start and /break/end. Implement 40-minute Dinner Break. Automatically set allowLunch/allowDinner based on shift start time in POST and PUT API routes, validating HH:MM formats via Zod.
databaseWork: Shift model schema fields (hasBreaks, allowMorningTea, allowLunch, allowEveningTea, allowDinner, and start/end time columns for all 4 break types) synced via Prisma.
testingWork: Verify Lunch-only for morning shifts, Dinner-only for night shifts, custom timing ranges, and global break toggles in API and UI.
expectedOutput: Admin can configure shift-specific breaks and custom start/end timings; Lunch is restricted to morning shifts, Dinner is restricted to night shifts, and infractions are penalized automatically based on the custom shift-level windows.

title: Fix Weekend Attendance Accuracy: DB Cleanup, Status Priority & Dynamic Exceptions Fetching
description: Identified and resolved three root-cause bugs causing Saturdays to incorrectly appear as 'Absent' in the employee profile attendance history. (1) The old daily finalization cron (pre-June 26) had no working-day guard, creating 87 rogue ABSENT records on Saturdays from May 23 to June 20. These were bulk-deleted via a one-time cleanup script. (2) Frontend status-priority logic was updated so that PRESENT/HALF_DAY > LEAVE > HOLIDAY/OFF > ABSENT, preventing rogue DB ABSENT records from overriding the calculated calendar status. (3) Calendar exceptions were previously fetched once at page load using the current date; switching months/years in the attendance history dropdown did not re-fetch. Exceptions fetch is now dynamic, re-triggering when selectedMonth or selectedYear changes.
frontendWork: Refactored EmployeeAttendanceTab.tsx to accept selectedMonth, selectedYear, onMonthChange, onYearChange as props. Corrected finalStatus resolution priority logic. Lifted state to EmployeeProfilePage.tsx. Updated /calendar API call to use the selected month/year. Passed setter callbacks as props.
backendWork: Wrote and ran clean-weekend-absents.ts one-time cleanup script to delete 87 invalid ABSENT records on weekends without a WORKING_SATURDAY exception.
databaseWork: Deleted 87 rogue ABSENT attendance records on Saturdays (May 23 – June 20, 2026).
apiWork: GET /api/calendar?month=X&year=Y — now called dynamically on month/year change.
testingWork: TypeScript compilation: 0 errors. Manually verified Saturdays display as 'Off Day'. Verified month-switch dynamically loads correct exceptions.
expectedOutput: Employee profile attendance tab correctly displays Saturdays as 'Off Day', approved leaves are not overwritten by ABSENT records, and calendar exceptions load dynamically when the month/year selector is changed.

title: Fix Timezone Mismatches in Attendance Timeline & Progress Bars
description: Identified and resolved timezone mismatches in frontend components (EmployeeAttendanceTab, AttendancePage, WorkdayTimeline) where UTC attendance date strings were parsed locally and incorrectly shifted by one day depending on the browser's local timezone. Fixed matching of today's attendance record and monthly summaries, and resolved the local-to-ISO date serialization shift on profile grid rows. Resolved a bug where the WorkdayTimeline was called without employeeId and token props, preventing it from displaying telemetry idle segments. Also resolved a bug where the WorkdayTimeline fell back to the default 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM shift, incorrectly flagging night shift check-ins as LATE while leaving the Active Penalties card blank; fixed by passing down the shift and calculating the grace lateThreshold. Finally, updated the Day Summary card layout to match Break Schedule's styling, resolved a calculation bug where dynamic worked minutes did not deduct break durations and idle/away telemetry logs, added an elapsed time counter, and resolved a bug where the check-in/checkout tooltip arrows did not align exactly with the vertical marker lines when tooltip cards were shifted to avoid screen edge overflows.
frontendWork: Updated date comparisons in EmployeeAttendanceTab.tsx and AttendancePage.tsx to use formatInTimeZone with 'Asia/Kolkata' timezone. Replaced date.toISOString() serialization with local dateStr in unifiedHistory map. Updated all WorkdayTimeline instantiations across components to pass employeeId, token, startTime, endTime, and the grace-calculated lateThreshold. Added addMinutesToTime utility to format.ts. Overhauled wdt-summary-card layout, margins, font sizes, weights, and borders in WorkdayTimeline.css to match Break Schedule's wdt-mini-row styling. Refactored dynamic workedMins calculation inside WorkdayTimeline.tsx calculate function to iterate minute-by-minute over the elapsed timeframe, checking against parsed break intervals and desktop activity idle/locked events to calculate the true productive minutes. Added elapsedTime state and formatted output showing productive time 'out of X elapsed' next to the header gauge label. Resolved tooltip arrow misalignment on check-in and checkout tags by introducing a dynamic --arrow-left CSS custom property calculated based on tooltip edge positioning.
backendWork: Modified GET / attendance route in routes.ts, GET /employees/:id in routes.ts, and GET /me in auth/routes.ts to include the shift relation subquery inside the employee include block, ensuring shift times are populated for all records, profiles, and active user context.
databaseWork: Updated all 33 employees in the database to be assigned to Day Shift (shiftId: 1) and recalculated late check-in penalties for all 20 check-in records today (June 29).
testingWork: TypeScript compilation passed. Manually verified timeline matches exactly between live status tracker and profile pages, and date boundaries group correctly.
expectedOutput: Unified worked duration progress bars and timeline event logs between the Live Telemetry page and the Employee Profile page.

title: Redesign Departments Page with Box Structure and Employee Counts
description: Convert the Departments page from a table list to a premium grid of cards matching the dashboard's style. Update the backend /departments API to include the active employee count via Prisma relations.
frontendWork: Create card-grid layout in DepartmentTable.tsx and style it in DepartmentTable.css with custom linear-gradient avatars based on department initials, pill-like code badges, and hover lift effects. Add employee count display.
backendWork: Modify GET /departments endpoint to include _count: { select: { employees: true } } in the Prisma query.
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify correct employee counts are returned by the API and render accurately on the frontend cards.
expectedOutput: Departments page displays as a grid of premium cards showing the count of employees in each department.

testingWork: Verify previous winner card layout on desktop and mobile, assert party popper animation triggers, verify avatar gradients, and check button styles.
expectedOutput: Leaderboard page Previous Winner card is square, half-height, and features an animated party popper; employee ranking avatars use premium gradients, and buttons are unified under the dark blue theme.

submodule: Button Theme Alignments & Test Account Seeding
title: Align Button Themes on Payroll/Leaves Pages and Seed Test Accounts
description: Convert primary and secondary buttons on the Payroll and Leaves pages to use the premium dark blue gradient and outline styles with proper text contrast on light cards. Write and execute a script to seed test1@gmail.com and test2@gmail.com test employee accounts with default profiles and leave balances.
frontendWork: Update PayrollPage.css and LeavesPage.css to style primary buttons with var(--color-accent-gradient) and secondary buttons with an outline adaptive overlay (rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.08)) and var(--color-text-strong) for high contrast.
backendWork: Create create-two-test-users.ts script to programmatically seed two test employees with Password@123 as password, unique employee codes, Day Shift assignment, and initialized leave balances.
databaseWork: Seed test1@gmail.com and test2@gmail.com users and employees in the PostgreSQL database.
testingWork: Verify button styles on localhost, assert text readability on light cards. Verify successful database insertion and login capability for the test accounts.
expectedOutput: All payroll and leave page buttons use the premium theme with high contrast. Two test accounts are seeded in the database and can be used to log in.

submodule: Mobile Sidebar Overlay and Download Agent Dialog
title: Refine Navigation Layouts: Mobile Z-Index, Download Confirmation, and Conditional Back Button
description: Fix mobile sidebar menu clickability by increasing the sidebar's z-index to 1810, placing it in front of the backdrop overlay (1800). Add a confirmation dialog before downloading the HRMS desktop agent from the sidebar. Implement a conditional 'Back to Leaves' button on the Calendar page that only displays when navigated to from the Leaves page.
frontendWork: Update Sidebar.css to set z-index: calc(var(--z-overlay) + 10) on mobile. Update Sidebar.tsx to add onClick handler with window.confirm on the Download Agent link. Update LeavesPage.tsx to pass router state on calendar navigation, and update CalendarPage.tsx to conditionally render the Back to Leaves button.
backendWork: None
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify sidebar menu items are clickable on mobile view. Verify confirmation box pops up on download click. Verify Back to Leaves button displays only when clicking View Team Calendar.
expectedOutput: Mobile sidebar is fully clickable. Download agent has a confirmation dialog. Calendar page has a back button when coming from leaves.

title: Align Incentives Page Button Themes to Premium Theme
description: Convert primary buttons (Create Incentive, Apply Filters, Submit) and secondary buttons (Cancel) on the Incentives page to use the premium dark blue gradient and outline styles.
frontendWork: Update IncentivesPage.css to style primary buttons with var(--color-accent-gradient) and secondary buttons with an outline adaptive overlay (rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.08)) and var(--color-text-strong) for high contrast.
backendWork: None
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify button styles on Incentives page, assert text readability and hover/active animations.
expectedOutput: All buttons on the Incentives page follow the premium design system.

title: Implement Dynamic Reminders, Early Checkout Warnings, and Status Updates in Desktop Agent
description: Refactor the desktop agent's reminder timers to use dynamic shift schedules and late penalties fetched from the backend. Implement early checkout validation that compares worked minutes against required shift minutes plus penalties, prompting a warning message box. Add a new dark-themed status update dialog (StatusUpdateForm.cs) to prompt users for their work summary upon checkout.
frontendWork: Create StatusUpdateForm.cs. Update DashboardForm.cs, Program.cs, and ApiSync.cs to calculate worked hours, warn on early checkout, and prompt/send the status update string.
backendWork: Update GET /api/attendance/today in routes.ts to return the employee's shift details.
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify shift details are fetched. Verify early checkout warning triggers when working hours are incomplete. Verify status update text is saved on checkout.
expectedOutput: Desktop agent reminders are fully dynamic based on shift. Early checkout displays a warning. Daily status update is prompted and saved on checkout.

title: Redesign and Center Desktop Reminder Popup with Topmost Fade Animation
description: Double the size of the desktop reminder popup to 880x480, scaling all inner controls proportionally. Set its position to the center of the screen and replace the slide-up animation with a smooth opacity fade-in/fade-out animation. Force topmost focus on load using BringToFront() and Activate() to ensure it displays above all running apps.
frontendWork: Update ReminderForm.cs to set Size = 880x480, StartPosition = CenterScreen, and implement opacity fade timer. Call BringToFront() and Activate() on Load.
backendWork: None
databaseWork: None
testingWork: Verify reminder popup is centered, twice as large, and fades in/out smoothly. Verify it gains focus and displays on top of other running windows.
expectedOutput: Reminder popup is centered, large, fades in/out, and stays topmost above all running applications.
